title: mentha arvensis var. borealis
intro: Mentha arvensis var. borealis, also known as wild mint, is a variant of the mint plant species that is native to the northern hemisphere. It is characterized by its square stem and green leaves with purple flowers, and is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to aid in digestion and alleviate nausea. The menthol content in wild mint has been traditionally used to soothe respiratory issues, such as coughs and colds, and to reduce inflammation. Wild mint can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or essential oil, and is often used in herbal remedies to promote relaxation, relieve headaches, and support overall well-being.
